creator: Anthony Ha
title: (No AI) New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
dc:creator: Anthony Ha
content: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?
contentSnippet: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

creator: Anthony Ha
title: (No AI) Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
dc:creator: Anthony Ha
content: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.
contentSnippet: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

title: (No AI) Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![The founding fathers having a conference call with all of their cameras off via Google Meet, as if they wouldn’t be a bunch of stodgy Microsoft Teams users.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Q3RjZY-rSsc-HD.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

I call BS: the founding fathers definitely would have been Microsoft Teams users. | Image: Google

"Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for [Google Workspace](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q3RjZY-rSsc&t=65s) opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence.

Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to check on the status of a draft, who takes a photo and uses AI to transcribe it into a Google Doc. Franklin and Adams hop in to make edits in suggestion mode, Gemini finds them a meeting time, takes notes during a Google Meet call, and then Nano Banana whips up a seal for the United States featuring a turkey ( …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/961468/google-ai-commercial-founding-fathers-declaration-of-independence)

title: (No AI) The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Hands with too many fingers typing on a keyboard.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/AI_Hands_A_Bernis_02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.

[Broad distaste](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/897900/ai-trust-gap-killer-app-vergecast) around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been _a thing_ in [creative communities](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/864951/human-artistry-campaign-ai-licensing-artists), including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the [broad concept](https://x.com/ffe2ff/status/2070804900871634965) of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called [@heatedrivalryai](https://x.com/heatedrivalryai) promised a seemingly more reliable solution …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/960854/ai-fanfiction-ao3-claude-detector)

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.
contentSnippet: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

title: (No AI) Google DeepMind Unionization Talks Are Off to a Rocky Start
dc:creator: Joel Khalili
content: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.
contentSnippet: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Can Cursor Remain a Platform for OpenAI and Anthropic’s Models Inside SpaceX?
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
contentSnippet: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
